questions: What are geo‑experiment basics for incrementality?
When should we use geo tests instead of user‑level RCTs?
qa.answer: Geo experiments randomize at the geography level (markets/regions) to measure incremental lift when user‑level randomization is impractical. They leverage pre‑post controls and synthetic controls to estimate treatment effects.
implication: Choose geo testing for channels without user‑level controls (e.g., TV, OOH).
Ensure enough geos and stable baselines; stratify by size and seasonality.
Use CUPED/synthetic controls to reduce variance and shorten duration.
